description: YAML closely mirrors BSV structure. Each BSV construct has a corresponding YAML representation. L4-level explicit code snippets in knowledge.bsv.code. Compiler extracts code directly, no LLM needed for generation.
yaml_style: L4 code snippets: complete BSV source embedded in YAML knowledge fields
compiler_strategy: Direct extraction from knowledge.bsv.code → write to .bsv files
result: FPC=1.0 (3/3 compile), ID=1.186, simulation blocked by upstream test infrastructure

description: Reduce abstraction level from L4 to L2-L3. YAML describes types, enums, structs, and functions in natural language (NOT via code snippets). LLM agent generates BSV from abstract descriptions. Target: better ID ratio and higher abstraction level.
yaml_style: L2-L3 level: natural language descriptions of types, enums, structures, functions. No embedded BSV code except for Settings typedefs (which are inherently code-level).
compiler_strategy: Agent-based LLM generation: spawn agents to read YAML and generate BSV files in parallel
result: ID=0.43 (50.7% reduction from iter_001), but BSV generation from L2-L3 YAML stalled — agents didn't produce usable output

description: Bypass Utils4Test dependency chain by creating standalone minimal testbenches for each module. Enables real TPR/ZFPR measurement without needing full test infrastructure to compile.
yaml_style: Using iter_001 generated BSV (L4-based), with standalone minimal testbenches that test module behavior independently
compiler_strategy: Workspace-based compilation with standalone testbenches. Each module tested in isolation with explicit type declarations to avoid BSC T0031/T0035 errors.
result: FPC=1.0, TPR=1.0 (3/3), ZFPR=1.0 — first real ZFPR achievement! All 3 modules pass simulation with standalone testbenches

description: Implement Two-Phase compiler with centralized type registry. Phase 1: Scan all module YAMLs → generate types.bsv with all typedefs/enums/structs/interfaces. Phase 2: Generate module BSV bottom-up from YAML descriptions. Scale to all 20 test modules with batch parallel testing.
yaml_style: L2-L3 level with centralized types.yaml registry. Type definitions centralized, module YAML references types by ID. Interface contracts described at L2, behavior at L3.
compiler_strategy: Two-Phase: Phase 1 (Types): extract types from all YAMLs → types.bsv → Phase 2 (Modules): bottom-up dependency order, generate each module's BSV from its YAML using rule-based code generation. Parallel test execution.
